config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar mayoritas tidak semena-mena terhadap orang yang lemah atau berbeda. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial, ketika hak pribadi terlalu mudah dikorbankan atas nama kepentingan bersama, yang sering terjadi justru penyalahgunaan kuasa dan ketidakadilan. Jadi, yang tepat adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat selalu di atas individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we are deeply shaped by the idea that rights exist precisely to protect people from being steamrolled by the majority or by the state. Public safety, public health, and basic order do matter, but once "society" becomes a blanket excuse, it can justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power. I think the better principle is that individual rights are the default, and society can limit them only when there is a clear, narrow, and serious reason. In practice, that balance is what keeps a free society from turning into tyranny of the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis ditempatkan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat kondisi di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, menutup ruang kritik, atau membenarkan tindakan yang merugikan kelompok rentan. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya secara terbatas, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan dijadikan alasan umum untuk mengalahkan martabat orang per orang. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu yang kuat, lalu kepentingan bersama dicapai tanpa mengorbankan keadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse overreach by government or by the majority. The claim that "the public good" should come first can be valid in narrow cases like emergency health rules or criminal law, but only when the limits are specific, necessary, and accountable. I also think the previous point about abuse of the phrase "for the common good" is important, because once rights become conditional on what society wants, minorities and unpopular people are usually the first to suffer. A free society should protect individual rights first and then justify any restriction with a very strong reason, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering jadi jalan pintas untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau melegitimasi tindakan sewenang-wenang, jadi saya tidak percaya kalau itu dijadikan prinsip utama. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang kuat, jelas, dan bisa diuji, karena tanpa itu yang menang biasanya bukan masyarakat, melainkan pihak yang paling berkuasa. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir dari perlindungan individu yang tegas, bukan dari pengorbanan hak orang per orang atas nama mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual, especially in the United States where history shows how easily “the common good” can become a cover for overreach. The point about minorities and critics being the first to suffer is exactly why I am wary of any principle that puts collective interests above basic rights by default. Public needs matter, but they should justify only narrow, accountable limits, not a general rule that lets the majority override unpopular people. In my view, a healthy society is one that earns collective action without treating individual rights as expendable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
